text:
Steiner (シュタイナー, Shutainā) is one of the highest-ranked individuals in the International Permit Agency (IPA). Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 341 After the V6 approved Kakin's expedition to the Dark Continent, he was tasked with acting as Beyond Netero's guide. For the duration of the voyage, Steiner and several other IPA members have been placed on loan to Kakin's Justice Bureau. 

Appearance

Steiner is a tall, bespectacled man. He has light brown hair and hazel eyes. He tends to dress in a suit and tie for official business occasions.

Personality

In his introduction, Steiner appeared to be a self-confident man. However, he became quickly aghast after entering the International Permit Agency basement and learning about Don Freecss' book and the Five Threats from the Dark Continent. After studying the Dark Continent further, he made a conclusion the Dark Continent is not a place for humans to return to. He nonetheless joins the voyage to the Dark Continent to monitor Kakin. 

In a meeting with the V5, Steiner shows his pragmatic and tactical sides by advising the world politicians to invite Kakin into V6 and endorse Kakin's project in order to let Nasubi Hui Guo Rou take all the fallout, but the benefits should be divided evenly if the project is successful. He also advised the ploy of labeling some uninhabited islands in the New Continent as the Dark Continent, so the public who joins the voyage will not actually be taken to the dangerous continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 342 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Steiner is taken to the basement of the International Environmental Permit Agency by the agency director, who tells that the team he has been put in charge of has the job of coordinating the Restricted Voyage Agencies. As they make their way to the basement, the director has Steiner recite the rules for his job. Steiner does so, and also demonstrates awareness of their implicit goal to prevent all travelers to the new world, satisfying the director. In the basement, Steiner is shocked to see the victims of the Five Threats, which the director explains were calamities humanity was forced to bring back during their previous attempts at colonizing the new world. The director has him read the East edition of Journey to the New World and prepare a report on the risks of passage to the new world. Later, Steiner watches a recording of Netero explaining the Hunter Association's history with the new world, which the Zodiacs also see elsewhere. As Steiner reads, he concludes the new world is not a place where humanity should go ever again, and has the Hunter Association commissioned with Beyond's capture. 

Sometime later, Steiner conducts a briefing with the heads of the V5. The heads cut his summary on humanity's past expeditions short, and asks Steiner how they could stop Kakin's expedition. Steiner establishes that the means to stop them are limited and military, and proposes an alternative: to invite Kakin into the V5 (thus reorganizing into the V6), support their journey, find an uninhabited island sufficiently far away to act as the new continent for its civilians, and let Kakin continue on the actual voyage to suffer the new world themselves. After Steiner answers some further questions, the heads of the V5 agree to his proposal. After the presentation, the director congratulates him, and Steiner confesses he is conflicted about the morality of his actions. Still, the director replies that a world at peace is more important than honesty. Hearing this garnishes praise from his superior director, who claims he'll be a great director someday. 

The V6 later appoints Steiner as Beyond Netero's guide. On the day the Black Whale departs, Steiner flips through a heavily-tagged book and advises Peuckert and his other cabinmates to at least read the chapter on the labyrinth city (where the "Botanical Weapon" Brion is known to be found), since one of them will have to take his place as deputy secretary of the IPA if he dies. However, Steiner's cabinmates are confident of their chances of survival, since the Hunter Association is in charge of security and the current Director of the IPA is one of the unofficial "sole survivors" (of trips to the Dark Continent). 

On the 10 th day of the voyage, Balsamilco has 9 th Prince Halkenburg arrested on suspicion of premediated murder; RVA members Steiner and Peuckert (currently on loan to the Kakin Justice Bureau Investigation Department) accompany the Kakin army to arrest Halkenburg. Halkenburg does not resist arrest, and Steiner and company bring him away, separating him from his men until his trial is finished 

Later in the day, Steiner witnesses Kakin Justice Bureau Investigator Kaiser see "Kacho" At this time, 10 th Prince Kacho Hui Guo Rou had already died and been replaced by Without You, her Guardian Spirit Beast. off to the VIP Witness Protection Area from the entrance of the Investigation Rooms, all within the Justice Bureau on Tier 2. After "Kacho" leaves, Kaiser turns to Steiner and asks him for a moment of his time. Kaiser gives Steiner a button, and then proceeds to lower his voice and give him instructions to press the button if the 1 st Prince Benjamin's men or the Kakin Royal Army appear at the Justice Bureau. He explains to Steiner that the declaration of special martial law It is explained in Chapter 400 that Special Martial Law for the Kakin Empire during the Succession Contest is when the military temporarily takes control of the powers of government in the case of a national emergency. This is very similar to martial law in the real world. would turn even the Justice Bureau (the most neutral institution on the ship), into a dictator's base. He explains the reasoning behind his instructions by highlighting his worst-case scenario to Steiner, that the transfer to military power happens right after a large amount of Kakin Royal Army soldiers are sent inside the Justice Bureau, which would allow the Kakin Royal Army to execute anyone within the Justice Bureau that they deem as an insurgent within the short span of ten minutes. Not being privy to the true nature of the Succession Contest, Steiner asks if the situation is really so dire that the army might go rogue. Kaiser does not know, but explains that the button is the last check against the army going rogue, and that giving Steiner the button is a precaution to maximize the chances of the button being pressed in case of special martial law, taking advantage of Steiner's status as one of the few "witnesses" on board the Black Whale that is guaranteed to return alive. Steiner worries that him being handed the button means a chance of him dying despite his protected status, and further considers that his death might be a result of a scenario that cannot be officially recorded happening. Steiner chooses not to voice his thoughts, and merely acknowledges his senior's instructions. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

On the eleventh day of the voyage, Steiner, together with Peuckert and another colleague from the IPA, joins Kaiser's escort of 11 th Prince Fugetsu throughout Tier 1 to deliver a letter from her recently-deceased sister 10 th Prince Kacho to several of her fellow Princes on Tier 1. Steiner is seen as part of the group escorting Fugetsu to the then-residences of 1 st Prince Benjamin, 5 th Prince Tubeppa, 6 th Prince Tyson, and 7 th Prince Luzurus. However, he is not seen in the presence of the Princes themselves. After Fugetsu receives a good luck charm from Luzurus' guard Basho on their way out of Room 1007, Steiner sees Kaiser bow in appreciation to Basho, unaware that the good luck charm Basho had given Fugetsu had temporarily warded off the low-level Nen spirits surrounding her. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 

Later that day, Steiner speaks with Kaiser about the mass syncope episode that Halkenburg had notified them about. Kaiser says that he knows about the report saying that about a dozen of Halkenburg's supporters had passed out as he warned them. Steiner takes the chance to bring up something he found concerning that another staff who was there during the visitation period had passed out with the same symptoms, but at a different time and place. At Kaiser's request, Steiner identifies him as Worio Bay, currently waiting at his quarters in Room E-6 ostensibly for observation. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Abilities & Powers

Steiner has vast authority over the International Permit Agency and all the RVAs. His position has allowed him to communicate directly with the V5 leaders at least once, an occasion in which he showed to be a capable public speaker: his speech influenced their course of action, leading to the approval of King Nasubi's colonization project and the nominal creation of the New Continent, preventing an armed conflict. Having read the East edition of Journey to the New World, Steiner was deemed knowledgeable enough to act as Beyond's guide in the Dark Continent. 

Manga Appearances

Quotes

(To the IPA Director, who inquired about his duties) "Don't let anybody go to the new world. When you have to, keep them on a short leash." 
(To the V5) "Considering the above, to quell this situation peacefully it is necessary to invite Kakin to the V5! Reorganize as the V6, and unofficially support Kakin's journey." 
(To the V5) "Let them suffer the same fate that united the V5 so strongly in the first place." 
(To the IPA Director) "Our official stance is that Journey to the New World was fiction, but in actuality, that book is the basis for the most important criteria for the world..." 

Trivia

Steiner is named after Felix Steiner, a German SS commander. He shares a similar origin of his name (a reference to known German figures) with Kaiser and Peuckert.
Steiner is only named in Chapter 384, 33 chapters after his introduction in Chapter 341. Interestingly, Kaiser and Peuckert also have a long delay between being introduced and being named, 28 chapters for Kaiser and 25 chapters for Peuckert.
